content:
Key Findings

  • Wyre Council spent £683.0M across 51,092 transactions from 2017/18 to 2025/26
  • Spending increased by £74.6M (107.9%) from 2017 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £120.5M (235.8%) from 2022 to 2023
  • The largest year-over-year decrease was £99.458M (58.0%) from 2023 to 2024
  • Wyre Council worked with 1,563 unique suppliers over the 8-year period

Supplier Retention Patterns

Wyre Council's spending data reveals a complex picture of supplier relationships. Over the 8-year period, the council worked with 1,563 unique suppliers. This large number suggests a mix of long-term partners and one-time vendors. The data shows significant fluctuations in spending year-over-year, which may indicate changes in supplier relationships or project-based spending.

The largest year-over-year increase in spending was £120.5M (235.8%) from 2022 to 2023. This substantial rise could suggest a major new contract or a series of large procurements. Conversely, the largest decrease was £99.458M (58.0%) from 2023 to 2024. Such fluctuations may indicate the completion of large projects or changes in supplier contracts.

Year-Over-Year Spending Analysis

Wyre Council's spending has seen significant variations over the years. The overall spending increased by £74.6M (107.9%) from 2017 to 2025. However, the year-over-year changes tell a more nuanced story. For example, spending dropped by £60,361,039 (-87.3%) from 2017 to 2018, then increased by £9.1M (104.1%) from 2018 to 2019. These fluctuations suggest that the council's spending patterns are influenced by specific projects or external factors.

The data also shows that the council's spending can vary significantly from year to year. For instance, spending increased by £85.6M (479.3%) from 2019 to 2020, then decreased by £58,054,763 (-56.1%) from 2020 to 2021. These changes may reflect the impact of external events or shifts in the council's priorities.

Top Suppliers and Spending

While the data does not provide a breakdown of the top suppliers, the overall spending patterns suggest that Wyre Council works with a diverse range of vendors. The large number of unique suppliers indicates that the council may prefer to distribute its spending across multiple providers rather than relying on a few key partners. This approach can help ensure competition and potentially better value for money.

The significant year-over-year changes in spending also suggest that the council may be responsive to market conditions and external factors. For example, the large increase in spending from 2022 to 2023 may indicate a response to inflation or changes in the cost of goods and services. Understanding these patterns can help residents and stakeholders better understand the council's procurement strategies.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Wyre Council about the specific suppliers involved in the largest year-over-year spending changes
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about the council's procurement strategies and supplier relationships
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see the details of major contracts and suppliers
  • Write to your local councillor about the fluctuations in spending and the impact on local suppliers
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse supplier relationships over time
This article is based on Wyre Council's published spending data covering 2017/18 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
